title: Termux Alternative: Why Acode Is the Better Android Development Environment | Acode
description: Looking for a Termux alternative? Acode combines the same Alpine Linux terminal with a professional code editor, AI coding support, Git, and 250+ plugins. The complete Code Editor Termux users upgrade to.
heroHeadline: The Termux Alternative with a Real Code Editor
heroSubheadline: Acode gives you the same proot-based Alpine Linux terminal as Termux — plus a professional code editor, AI coding agents, Git integration, and 250+ plugins. One app. No switching.
problemHeadline: Why Developers Look for Termux Alternatives
problemPoints: Termux is the most popular Linux terminal for Android. It gives you bash, apk, and access to thousands of packages. But Termux has one major limitation: it has no code editor.

When you're coding in Termux, you're stuck with terminal-based editors like Vim or Nano. On a touchscreen phone, Vim is painful. There's no syntax highlighting for web languages. No project file browser. No multi-file tabs.

Every Termux user eventually hits the same wall: you need to edit code, but you can't do it comfortably. You end up installing a separate code editor app and constantly switching between Termux (for the terminal) and another app (for editing).
There's been no app that combines the terminal power of Termux with a real modern code editor. That's exactly what Acode was built to solve.
solutionHeadline: Acode: The Same Terminal, Plus a Complete development environment
solutionPoints: Acode uses the same proot-based Alpine Linux as Termux. You get the same apk package manager, the same access to Alpine's repository, the same terminal commands. Nothing changes in your terminal workflow.

But Acode adds a professional code editor — Ace Editor v1.22.0 with syntax highlighting for 100+ languages, multi-file tabs, command palette (Ctrl+Shift+P), file search (Ctrl+P), keyboard shortcuts, and live HTML preview.

The killer feature: Acodes CLI. Run acode . in the terminal to open your current folder in the editor. Run acode file.js to open a specific file. The terminal and editor are connected — not separate apps.
And because Acode has a real terminal, you can run AI coding agents — Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode — right in the terminal, then view and edit AI-generated files in the editor. Termux can't do this.
setupHeadline: How to Switch from Termux to Acode

setupSteps.title: Install Acode
setupSteps.description: Get Acode from Google Play or F-Droid. It's free.

setupSteps.title: Set Up the Terminal
setupSteps.description: Tap the terminal icon. Acode downloads and configures Alpine Linux — the same as Termux. Takes about a minute.

setupSteps.title: Install Your Tools
setupSteps.description: Run apk update && apk add nodejs npm git python3 py3-pip to get your development environment set up. Install whatever you had in Termux.

setupSteps.title: Copy Your Projects
setupSteps.description: Use Git to clone your repos: git clone https://github.com/you/project.git. Or copy files from Termux's storage to Acode's workspace.

setupSteps.title: Configure Your Shell
setupSteps.description: Customize Acode's /initrc file just like you configured Termux's .bashrc. Aliases, environment variables, prompt customization — all supported.

setupSteps.title: Migrate Gradually
setupSteps.description: You can keep Termux installed and use both. Use Termux for your familiar terminal-only workflows and Acode for projects that benefit from the editor. Many developers use both.

features.title: Same Terminal, Better Editor
features.description: You keep the Alpine Linux terminal you know. You gain a professional editor with syntax highlighting, multi-file tabs, keyboard shortcuts, and project management. No compromises.

features.title: acode CLI — Bridge Terminal to Editor
features.description: The acode command opens files and folders from the terminal into Acode's editor. Navigate with ls and cd, open with acode file.js. Workflow is seamless.

features.title: AI Coding Ready
features.description: Run Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode in Acode's terminal, then edit the AI-generated output in the editor. Termux can run AI agents but has no editor to handle the output.

features.title: 250+ Community Plugins
features.description: Extend Acode with plugins for language support, themes, AI assistants, Git GUIs, and more. Termux uses packages for tools but has no plugin system for editor features.

features.title: Full Web Development
features.description: Edit HTML/CSS/JS with live preview. Debug with JavaScript console. Run build tools like webpack, vite, and npm scripts. Full web dev environment — something Termux alone can't provide.

features.title: One App, Not Two
features.description: Stop switching between Termux and a separate code editor. Acode has both. Less context switching, fewer taps, more productivity.

faqs.question: Does Acode's terminal work exactly like Termux?
faqs.answer: Yes. Both use proot-based Alpine Linux with the apk package manager. The terminal experience is identical — bash shell, customizable prompt, all standard Linux commands. If it works in Termux, it works in Acode.

faqs.question: Can I migrate my Termux configuration?
faqs.answer: You can manually copy configuration files (like .bashrc aliases) to Acode's /initrc file. Both environments use the same Alpine Linux base, so configurations are compatible.

faqs.question: Does Acode support all Termux packages?
faqs.answer: Acode uses Alpine Linux's apk repository, which contains thousands of packages. Most tools available in Termux are also available in Acode. Check with apk search <package>.

faqs.question: Why not just use Termux with a code editor app?
faqs.answer: Switching between two apps breaks your workflow. Acode's acode CLI command lets you open files from terminal to editor instantly. The integration saves time and cognitive load.

faqs.question: Is Acode faster than Termux?
faqs.answer: Both use the same proot technology, so terminal performance is similar. Acode adds editor features that use additional resources but are optimized for Android.

faqs.question: Can I contribute plugins to Acode?
faqs.answer: Yes! Acode has an open plugin system. Visit docs.acode.app for plugin development documentation. Plugins are written in JavaScript/TypeScript.
